query: WHO Ebola DRC 2026 outbreak Mongbwalu Rwampara cases deaths latest situation report
answer:
The research agent returned comprehensive findings. Let me compile the full report.

---

# WHO Ebola DRC 2026 Outbreak — Mongbwalu & Rwampara: Research Report

## Overview

This is the **17th Ebola outbreak** in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), caused by the **Bundibugyo ebolavirus** — only the third known outbreak of this strain (after Uganda 2007–08 and DRC 2012). It was officially declared on **15 May 2026** by DRC's Ministry of Health following laboratory confirmation by INRB in Kinshasa. WHO Director-General declared it a **Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC)** on **16 May 2026**.

## Key Epidemiological Data (as of 18–20 May 2026)

| Metric | Value | Source / Date |
|---|---|---|
| Suspected cases | 536–600+ | CARE (19 May); Project HOPE (20 May) |
| Probable cases | 105 | CARE (19 May) |
| Confirmed cases | 34–35 | CARE (19 May); Project HOPE (20 May) |
| Deaths (suspected + confirmed) | 134–139+ | CARE (19 May); Project HOPE (20 May) |
| Imported cases (Uganda) | 2 confirmed (1 death) | WHO SitRep 01 (18 May) |
| Case growth rate | 246 → 500 suspected in 96 hours (103% increase) | IRC (19 May) |
| Historical Bundibugyo CFR | 25–40% | WHO / historical data |
| Current crude CFR (suspected) | ~25% | Derived (134/536) |

## Affected Areas

### DRC — Ituri Province (epicenter)
- **Mongbwalu Health Zone** — the outbreak epicenter; a mining area with high population mobility
- **Rwampara Health Zone** — early affected area
- **Bunia Health Zone** — includes provincial capital Bunia

### DRC — North Kivu Province
- Cases confirmed including in **Goma**, a densely populated transit hub

### Cross-Border
- **Uganda**: 2 confirmed imported cases (confirmed 15–16 May; 1 death on 14 May)
- **Burundi, Rwanda, South Sudan**: Heightened readiness/alert

## Timeline

| Date | Event |
|---|---|
| ~29 March 2026 | First signs of epidemic observed in Bunia region (Caritas) |
| Early April 2026 | Outbreak likely began; undetected transmission for weeks |
| Late April 2026 | Presumed index case: a nurse who died at Evangelical Medical Center, Bunia |
| 5 May 2026 | WHO alerted about unknown illness with high mortality in Mongbwalu; 4 health workers died within 4 days |
| 9–10 May 2026 | MSF received first alerts of suspected viral hemorrhagic fever deaths |
| 14 May 2026 | First Uganda case died |
| 15 May 2026 | INRB confirmed Bundibugyo virus; DRC officially declared 17th Ebola outbreak; Uganda confirmed first imported case |
| 16 May 2026 | Uganda confirmed second imported case; WHO DG declared PHEIC |
| 17 May 2026 | WHO activated highest internal alert level |
| 18 May 2026 | WHO Weekly External Situation Report 01 data cutoff |
| 19 May 2026 | IRC reported 103% case surge in 96 hours |

## Vaccines & Therapeutics

- **No approved vaccine or treatment** exists for Bundibugyo ebolavirus (existing Ervebo/rVSV-ZEBOV vaccines target only the Zaire strain)
- Emergency clinical trial discussions underway at WHO
- **PCR diagnostic cartridges** for Bundibugyo virus available in insufficient quantities, slowing confirmation

## Response

- **WHO**: Delivered 11.5 tonnes of medical supplies within 72 hours; 35+ experts deployed
- **IFRC**: Launched CHF 29 million (~USD 34 million) regional emergency appeal; 200 Red Cross volunteers deployed
- **MSF**: Mobilizing medical and logistics teams; dispatching supplies
- **CARE**: Targeting 500,000–1 million people in affected/high-risk areas
- **MONUSCO**: Providing airlift support

## Contributing Factors to Severity

- **US funding cuts** (from March 2025) reduced IRC programming in Ituri from 5 to 2 health areas, weakening surveillance in the outbreak epicenter
- Weeks of **undetected transmission** before official declaration
- Ongoing **conflict and insecurity** in eastern DRC
- **Highly mobile populations** (mining, cross-border trade)
- **Healthcare worker infections/deaths** early in the outbreak
- Critical **PPE shortages** in health facilities
- Community attribution of deaths to **mystical causes**, hindering public health measures

---

key_facts: This is the 17th Ebola outbreak in DRC, caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus (only the 3rd known outbreak of this strain)
Outbreak officially declared 15 May 2026 by DRC Ministry of Health after INRB lab confirmation
WHO Director-General declared it a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) on 16 May 2026
Epicenter is Mongbwalu Health Zone in Ituri Province; Rwampara and Bunia health zones also affected
As of 19-20 May 2026: 536-600+ suspected cases, 105 probable cases, 34-35 confirmed cases, 134-139+ deaths
Cases confirmed in North Kivu Province including Goma, a major transit hub
2 confirmed imported cases in Uganda (1 death), confirmed 15-16 May 2026
Suspected cases surged from 246 to 500 in just 96 hours (103% increase) as of 19 May 2026
No approved vaccine or treatment exists for Bundibugyo ebolavirus; existing Ebola vaccines target only the Zaire strain
Outbreak likely began in early April 2026 but went undetected for weeks; first signs observed ~29 March 2026
US government funding cuts from March 2025 reduced IRC programming in Ituri from 5 to 2 health areas, weakening surveillance
WHO delivered 11.5 tonnes of medical supplies within 72 hours and deployed 35+ experts
IFRC launched CHF 29 million (~USD 34 million) regional emergency appeal
PCR diagnostic cartridges for Bundibugyo virus available in insufficient quantities, slowing case confirmation
4 health workers died within 4 days in the initial alert that reached WHO on 5 May 2026
